page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 22 vCPUs and 88 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. The instance is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz, configured with 11 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The processor architecture includes a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in vector and matrix operations, supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). In testing, the instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1623.79 and a multi-core score of 18300.51. Memory performance is highlighted by a cached read-only bandwidth of 1498.41 GB/sec and an uncached write speed of 46.01 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates high efficiency in text generation for small language models, reaching 312.98 tokens/sec, and handles static web serving at a throughput of 30.99 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-22 offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources, making it suitable for mainstream enterprise workloads without the overhead of specialized hardware. The presence of dedicated physical cores ensures predictable performance for multi-threaded applications. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as database operations, software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 22 vCPUs and 88 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 11 physical cores, it features advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage. Benchmark results show a stress-ng multi-core score of 18300.51 and a static web serving throughput of 30.99 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, achieving 312.98 tokens/sec. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring predictable compute performance.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose instance featuring 22 vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for enterprise workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose instance with 22 vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for predictable enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Features 22 dedicated vCPUs and 88 GB of RAM on an x86_64 architecture.
Powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 105 MB L3 cache.
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector math.
Delivers 30.99 GB/sec static web serving throughput and 24.14 million Redis SET operations/sec.
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and small-scale LLM inference.
